model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Kansas ranks 21st nationally in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +24.48 and boasts a top-10 defense (AdjD 94.0), which should overwhelm California Baptist's below-average offense (ORtg 107.1, 237th nationally). While the Jayhawks have been inconsistent down the stretch at 4-5 in their last nine games and dealt with Darryn Peterson's injury saga, Peterson has been cleared to play and Kansas's talent floor remains far above what Cal Baptist can match. The Lancers are making their first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance after winning the WAC Tournament, but their weak schedule (SOS 213th) and modest SRS of 2.52 suggest they are significantly outclassed by a Big 12 program with elite defensive metrics and a projected top-1 NBA draft pick on the roster.

reasoningStep.title: Kansas vs California Baptist – East 4/13
reasoningStep.summary: Kansas's elite defense and significant talent advantage should comfortably handle Cal Baptist's first-ever tournament appearance, despite the Jayhawks' late-season inconsistency.
evidence: Cal Baptist 25-8 from WAC, SRS 2.52 (130th), SOS -2.93 (213th)
Cal Baptist ORtg 107.1 (237th), DRtg 99.1 (32nd) – strong defense but weak offense
Darryn Peterson cleared to play for Kansas in NCAA Tournament
Cal Baptist making first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance after WAC title win over Utah Valley 63-61
Kansas went 4-5 in last 9 games but retains massive quality edge over a mid-major 13-seed

result.title: 2025-26 California Baptist Lancers men's basketball team
result.snippet: The 2025-26 California Baptist Lancers men's basketball team represents California Baptist University during the 2025-26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season. The Lancers , led by 13th-year head coach Rick Croy, play their home games at Fowler Events Center in Riverside, California , as members of the Western Athletic Conference ( WAC ).

I have enough information to make my analysis. Let me summarize what I know:

**Kansas (4-seed):**
- Record: 23-10, Big 12
- KenPom: #21, AdjEM +24.48, AdjO 118.4 (57th), AdjD 94.0
- Torvik: #22, AdjOE 117.95 (56th), AdjDE 94.38 (10th)
- Elite defense (top 10 nationally)
- Darryn Peterson (projected #1 NBA draft pick) cleared to play but had injury struggles during the season
- Ended regular season 4-5 in last 9 games, lost to Houston in Big 12 Tournament semis
- 23-10 record is underwhelming for a Kansas team

**California Baptist (13-seed):**
- Record: 25-8, WAC
- First-ever NCAA Tournament appearance
- SRS: 2.52 (130th), SOS: -2.93 (213th)
- ORtg: 107.1 (237th), DRtg: 99.1 (32nd) - strong defense, weak offense
- WAC Tournament champions (beat Utah Valley 63-61 in dramatic fashion)
- PS/G: 73.0 (257th), PA/G: 67.6 (36th)

The gap is enormous. Kansas ranks 21st-22nd nationally with an AdjEM of ~+24, while Cal Baptist's SRS of 2.52 (130th) against a weak schedule (213th SOS) suggests they're likely in the 100-130 range in true quality. Kansas's elite defense (AdjD ~94, top 10) should suffocate Cal Baptist's below-average offense (ORtg 107.1, 237th). Even with Kansas's late-season struggles and the Peterson drama, the talent and quality gap is too large for a 13-seed from the WAC making their first-ever tournament appearance.

Historically, 4-seeds beat 13-seeds about 79% of the time, but Kansas's quality metrics suggest they're stronger than a typical 4-seed.
